name: project-gin-framework
description: "Overview of the gin HTTP framework project — architecture, core types, and request lifecycle"

  node_type: memory

---

This is the [gin-gonic/gin](https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin) Go HTTP framework repository.

**Core types and locations:**
- `Engine` — gin.go:90, embeds RouterGroup, owns the radix tree forest and Context pool
- `RouterGroup` — routergroup.go:55, hierarchical route grouping with shared middleware/path prefix
- `Context` — context.go:59, per-request state; pooled via sync.Pool
- `HandlersChain` — gin.go:57, `[]HandlerFunc` — middleware + handler chain
- `node` (radix tree) — tree.go:99, supports static/param/catchAll node types

**Request lifecycle entry point:** `Engine.ServeHTTP` at gin.go:662

**Why:** Understanding architecture to support codebase navigation and feature work.
**How to apply:** Reference these locations when answering questions about routing, middleware, or request handling.

1	// Copyright 2014 Manu Martinez-Almeida. All rights reserved.
2	// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT style
3	// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
4	
5	package gin
6	
7	import (
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/mattn/go-isatty"
15	)
16	
17	type consoleColorModeValue int
18	
19	const (
20		autoColor consoleColorModeValue = iota
21		disableColor
22		forceColor
23	)
24	
25	const (
26		green   = "\033[97;42m"
27		white   = "\033[90;47m"
28		yellow  = "\033[90;43m"
29		red     = "\033[97;41m"
30		blue    = "\033[97;44m"
31		magenta = "\033[97;45m"
32		cyan    = "\033[97;46m"
33		reset   = "\033[0m"
34	)
35	
36	var consoleColorMode = autoColor
37	
38	// LoggerConfig defines the config for Logger middleware.
39	type LoggerConfig struct {
40		// Optional. Default value is gin.defaultLogFormatter
41		Formatter LogFormatter
42	
43		// Output is a writer where logs are written.
44		// Optional. Default value is gin.DefaultWriter.
45		Output io.Writer
46	
47		// SkipPaths is a URL path array which logs are not written.
48		// Optional.
49		SkipPaths []string
50	
51		// SkipQueryString indicates that query strings should not be written
52		// for cases such as when API keys are passed via query strings.
53		// Optional. Default value is false.
54		SkipQueryString bool
55	
56		// Skip is a Skipper that indicates which logs should not be written.
57		// Optional.
58		Skip Skipper
59	}
60	
61	// Skipper is a function to skip logs based on provided Context
62	type Skipper func(c *Context) bool
63	
64	// LogFormatter gives the signature of the formatter function passed to LoggerWithFormatter
65	type LogFormatter func(params LogFormatterParams) string
66	
67	// LogFormatterParams is the structure any formatter will be handed when time to log comes
68	type LogFormatterParams struct {
69		Request *http.Request
70	
71		// TimeStamp shows the time after the server returns a response.
72		TimeStamp time.Time
73		// StatusCode is HTTP response code.
74		StatusCode int
75		// Latency is how much time the server cost to process a certain request.
76		Latency time.Duration
77		// ClientIP equals Context's ClientIP method.
78		ClientIP string
79		// Method is the HTTP method given to the request.
80		Method string
81		// Path is a path the client requests.
82		Path string
83		// ErrorMessage is set if error has occurred in processing the request.
84		ErrorMessage string
85		// isTerm shows whether gin's output descriptor refers to a terminal.
86		isTerm bool
87		// BodySize is the size of the Response Body
88		BodySize int
89		// Keys are the keys set on the request's context.
90		Keys map[any]any
91	}
92	
93	// StatusCodeColor is the ANSI color for appropriately logging http status code to a terminal.
94	func (p *LogFormatterParams) StatusCodeColor() string {
95		code := p.StatusCode
96	
97		switch {
98		case code >= http.StatusContinue && code < http.StatusOK:
99			return white
100		case code >= http.StatusOK && code < http.StatusMultipleChoices:
101			return green
102		case code >= http.StatusMultipleChoices && code < http.StatusBadRequest:
103			return white
104		case code >= http.StatusBadRequest && code < http.StatusInternalServerError:
105			return yellow
106		default:
107			return red
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// LatencyColor is the ANSI color for latency
112	func (p *LogFormatterParams) LatencyColor() string {
113		latency := p.Latency
114		switch {
115		case latency < time.Millisecond*100:
116			return white
117		case latency < time.Millisecond*200:
118			return green
119		case latency < time.Millisecond*300:
120			return cyan
121		case latency < time.Millisecond*500:
122			return blue
123		case latency < time.Second:
124			return yellow
125		case latency < time.Second*2:
126			return magenta
127		default:
128			return red
129		}
130	}
131	
132	// MethodColor is the ANSI color for appropriately logging http method to a terminal.
133	func (p *LogFormatterParams) MethodColor() string {
134		method := p.Method
135	
136		switch method {
137		case http.MethodGet:
138			return blue
139		case http.MethodPost:
140			return cyan
141		case http.MethodPut:
142			return yellow
143		case http.MethodDelete:
144			return red
145		case http.MethodPatch:
146			return green
147		case http.MethodHead:
148			return magenta
149		case http.MethodOptions:
150			return white
151		default:
152			return reset
153		}
154	}
155	
156	// ResetColor resets all escape attributes.
157	func (p *LogFormatterParams) ResetColor() string {
158		return reset
159	}
160	
161	// IsOutputColor indicates whether can colors be outputted to the log.
162	func (p *LogFormatterParams) IsOutputColor() bool {
163		return consoleColorMode == forceColor || (consoleColorMode == autoColor && p.isTerm)
164	}
165	
166	// defaultLogFormatter is the default log format function Logger middleware uses.
167	var defaultLogFormatter = func(param LogFormatterParams) string {
168		var statusColor, methodColor, resetColor, latencyColor string
169		if param.IsOutputColor() {
170			statusColor = param.StatusCodeColor()
171			methodColor = param.MethodColor()
172			resetColor = param.ResetColor()
173			latencyColor = param.LatencyColor()
174		}
175	
176		switch {
177		case param.Latency > time.Minute:
178			param.Latency = param.Latency.Truncate(time.Second * 10)
179		case param.Latency > time.Second:
180			param.Latency = param.Latency.Truncate(time.Millisecond * 10)
181		case param.Latency > time.Millisecond:
182			param.Latency = param.Latency.Truncate(time.Microsecond * 10)
183		}
184	
185		return fmt.Sprintf("[GIN] %v |%s %3d %s|%s %8v %s| %15s |%s %-7s %s %#v\n%s",
186			param.TimeStamp.Format("2006/01/02 - 15:04:05"),
187			statusColor, param.StatusCode, resetColor,
188			latencyColor, param.Latency, resetColor,
189			param.ClientIP,
190			methodColor, param.Method, resetColor,
191			param.Path,
192			param.ErrorMessage,
193		)
194	}
195	
196	// DisableConsoleColor disables color output in the console.
197	func DisableConsoleColor() {
198		consoleColorMode = disableColor
199	}
200	
201	// ForceConsoleColor force color output in the console.
202	func ForceConsoleColor() {
203		consoleColorMode = forceColor
204	}
205	
206	// ErrorLogger returns a HandlerFunc for any error type.
207	func ErrorLogger() HandlerFunc {
208		return ErrorLoggerT(ErrorTypeAny)
209	}
210	
211	// ErrorLoggerT returns a HandlerFunc for a given error type.
212	func ErrorLoggerT(typ ErrorType) HandlerFunc {
213		return func(c *Context) {
214			c.Next()
215			errors := c.Errors.ByType(typ)
216			if len(errors) > 0 {
217				c.JSON(-1, errors)
218			}
219		}
220	}
221	
222	// Logger instances a Logger middleware that will write the logs to gin.DefaultWriter.
223	// By default, gin.DefaultWriter = os.Stdout.
224	func Logger() HandlerFunc {
225		return LoggerWithConfig(LoggerConfig{})
226	}
227	
228	// LoggerWithFormatter instance a Logger middleware with the specified log format function.
229	func LoggerWithFormatter(f LogFormatter) HandlerFunc {
230		return LoggerWithConfig(LoggerConfig{
231			Formatter: f,
232		})
233	}
234	
235	// LoggerWithWriter instance a Logger middleware with the specified writer buffer.
236	// Example: os.Stdout, a file opened in write mode, a socket...
237	func LoggerWithWriter(out io.Writer, notlogged ...string) HandlerFunc {
238		return LoggerWithConfig(LoggerConfig{
239			Output:    out,
240			SkipPaths: notlogged,
241		})
242	}
243	
244	// LoggerWithConfig instance a Logger middleware with config.
245	func LoggerWithConfig(conf LoggerConfig) HandlerFunc {
246		formatter := conf.Formatter
247		if formatter == nil {
248			formatter = defaultLogFormatter
249		}
250	
251		out := conf.Output
252		if out == nil {
253			out = DefaultWriter
254		}
255	
256		notlogged := conf.SkipPaths
257	
258		isTerm := true
259	
260		if w, ok := out.(*os.File); !ok || os.Getenv("TERM") == "dumb" ||
261			(!isatty.IsTerminal(w.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(w.Fd())) {
262			isTerm = false
263		}
264	
265		var skip map[string]struct{}
266	
267		if length := len(notlogged); length > 0 {
268			skip = make(map[string]struct{}, length)
269	
270			for _, path := range notlogged {
271				skip[path] = struct{}{}
272			}
273		}
274	
275		return func(c *Context) {
276			// Start timer
277			start := time.Now()
278			path := c.Request.URL.Path
279			raw := c.Request.URL.RawQuery
280	
281			// Process request
282			c.Next()
283	
284			// Log only when it is not being skipped
285			if _, ok := skip[path]; ok || (conf.Skip != nil && conf.Skip(c)) {
286				return
287			}
288	
289			param := LogFormatterParams{
290				Request: c.Request,
291				isTerm:  isTerm,
292				Keys:    c.Keys,
293			}
294	
295			// Stop timer
296			param.TimeStamp = time.Now()
297			param.Latency = param.TimeStamp.Sub(start)
298	
299			param.ClientIP = c.ClientIP()
300			param.Method = c.Request.Method
301			param.StatusCode = c.Writer.Status()
302			param.ErrorMessage = c.Errors.ByType(ErrorTypePrivate).String()
303	
304			param.BodySize = c.Writer.Size()
305	
306			if raw != "" && !conf.SkipQueryString {
307				path = path + "?" + raw
308			}
309	
310			param.Path = path
311	
312			fmt.Fprint(out, formatter(param))
313		}
314	}
315

90	// Engine is the framework's instance, it contains the muxer, middleware and configuration settings.
91	// Create an instance of Engine, by using New() or Default()
92	type Engine struct {
93		RouterGroup
94	
95		// routeTreesUpdated ensures that the initialization or update of the route trees
96		// (used for routing HTTP requests) happens only once, even if called multiple times concurrently.
97		routeTreesUpdated sync.Once
98	
99		// RedirectTrailingSlash enables automatic redirection if the current route can't be matched but a
100		// handler for the path with (without) the trailing slash exists.
101		// For example if /foo/ is requested but a route only exists for /foo, the
102		// client is redirected to /foo with http status code 301 for GET requests
103		// and 307 for all other request methods.
104		RedirectTrailingSlash bool
105	
106		// RedirectFixedPath if enabled, the router tries to fix the current request path, if no
107		// handle is registered for it.
108		// First superfluous path elements like ../ or // are removed.
109		// Afterwards the router does a case-insensitive lookup of the cleaned path.
110		// If a handle can be found for this route, the router makes a redirection
111		// to the corrected path with status code 301 for GET requests and 307 for
112		// all other request methods.
113		// For example /FOO and /..//Foo could be redirected to /foo.
114		// RedirectTrailingSlash is independent of this option.
115		RedirectFixedPath bool
116	
117		// HandleMethodNotAllowed if enabled, the router checks if another method is allowed for the
118		// current route, if the current request can not be routed.
119		// If this is the case, the request is answered with 'Method Not Allowed'
120		// and HTTP status code 405.
121		// If no other Method is allowed, the request is delegated to the NotFound
122		// handler.
123		HandleMethodNotAllowed bool
124	
125		// ForwardedByClientIP if enabled, client IP will be parsed from the request's headers that
126		// match those stored at `(*gin.Engine).RemoteIPHeaders`. If no IP was
127		// fetched, it falls back to the IP obtained from
128		// `(*gin.Context).Request.RemoteAddr`.
129		ForwardedByClientIP bool
130	
131		// AppEngine was deprecated.
132		// Deprecated: USE `TrustedPlatform` WITH VALUE `gin.PlatformGoogleAppEngine` INSTEAD
133		// #726 #755 If enabled, it will trust some headers starting with
134		// 'X-AppEngine...' for better integration with that PaaS.
135		AppEngine bool
136	
137		// UseRawPath if enabled, the url.RawPath will be used to find parameters.
138		// The RawPath is only a hint, EscapedPath() should be use instead. (https://pkg.go.dev/net/url@master#URL)
139		// Only use RawPath if you know what you are doing.
140		UseRawPath bool
141	
142		// UseEscapedPath if enable, the url.EscapedPath() will be used to find parameters
143		// It overrides UseRawPath
144		UseEscapedPath bool
145	
146		// UnescapePathValues if true, the path value will be unescaped.
147		// If UseRawPath and UseEscapedPath are false (by default), the UnescapePathValues effectively is true,
148		// as url.Path gonna be used, which is already unescaped.
149		UnescapePathValues bool
150	
151		// RemoveExtraSlash a parameter can be parsed from the URL even with extra slashes.
152		// See the PR #1817 and issue #1644
153		RemoveExtraSlash bool
154	
155		// RemoteIPHeaders list of headers used to obtain the client IP when
156		// `(*gin.Engine).ForwardedByClientIP` is `true` and
157		// `(*gin.Context).Request.RemoteAddr` is matched by at least one of the
158		// network origins of list defined by `(*gin.Engine).SetTrustedProxies()`.
159		RemoteIPHeaders []string
160	
161		// TrustedPlatform if set to a constant of value gin.Platform*, trusts the headers set by
162		// that platform, for example to determine the client IP
163		TrustedPlatform string
164	
165		// MaxMultipartMemory value of 'maxMemory' param that is given to http.Request's ParseMultipartForm
166		// method call.
167		MaxMultipartMemory int64
168	
169		// UseH2C enable h2c support.
170		UseH2C bool
171	
172		// ContextWithFallback enable fallback Context.Deadline(), Context.Done(), Context.Err() and Context.Value() when Context.Request.Context() is not nil.
173		ContextWithFallback bool
174	
175		delims           render.Delims
176		secureJSONPrefix string
177		HTMLRender       render.HTMLRender
178		FuncMap          template.FuncMap
179		allNoRoute       HandlersChain
180		allNoMethod      HandlersChain
181		noRoute          HandlersChain
182		noMethod         HandlersChain
183		pool             sync.Pool
184		trees            methodTrees
185		maxParams        uint16
186		maxSections      uint16
187		trustedProxies   []string
188		trustedCIDRs     []*net.IPNet
189	}

194	// By default, the configuration is:
195	// - RedirectTrailingSlash:  true
196	// - RedirectFixedPath:      false
197	// - HandleMethodNotAllowed: false
198	// - ForwardedByClientIP:    true
199	// - UseRawPath:             false
200	// - UseEscapedPath: 		 false
201	// - UnescapePathValues:     true
202	func New(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
203		debugPrintWARNINGNew()
204		engine := &Engine{
205			RouterGroup: RouterGroup{
206				Handlers: nil,
207				basePath: "/",
208				root:     true,
209			},
210			FuncMap:                template.FuncMap{},
211			RedirectTrailingSlash:  true,
212			RedirectFixedPath:      false,
213			HandleMethodNotAllowed: false,
214			ForwardedByClientIP:    true,
215			RemoteIPHeaders:        []string{"X-Forwarded-For", "X-Real-IP"},
216			TrustedPlatform:        defaultPlatform,
217			UseRawPath:             false,
218			UseEscapedPath:         false,
219			RemoveExtraSlash:       false,
220			UnescapePathValues:     true,
221			MaxMultipartMemory:     defaultMultipartMemory,
222			trees:                  make(methodTrees, 0, 9),
223			delims:                 render.Delims{Left: "{{", Right: "}}"},
224			secureJSONPrefix:       "while(1);",
225			trustedProxies:         []string{"0.0.0.0/0", "::/0"},
226			trustedCIDRs:           defaultTrustedCIDRs,
227		}
228		engine.engine = engine
229		engine.pool.New = func() any {
230			return engine.allocateContext(engine.maxParams)
231		}
232		return engine.With(opts...)
233	}

364	func (engine *Engine) addRoute(method, path string, handlers HandlersChain) {
365		assert1(path[0] == '/', "path must begin with '/'")
366		assert1(method != "", "HTTP method can not be empty")
367		assert1(len(handlers) > 0, "there must be at least one handler")
368	
369		debugPrintRoute(method, path, handlers)
370	
371		root := engine.trees.get(method)
372		if root == nil {
373			root = new(node)
374			root.fullPath = "/"
375			engine.trees = append(engine.trees, methodTree{method: method, root: root})
376		}
377		root.addRoute(path, handlers)
378	
379		if paramsCount := countParams(path); paramsCount > engine.maxParams {
380			engine.maxParams = paramsCount
381		}
382	
383		if sectionsCount := countSections(path); sectionsCount > engine.maxSections {
384			engine.maxSections = sectionsCount
385		}
386	}

388	// Routes returns a slice of registered routes, including some useful information, such as:
389	// the http method, path, and the handler name.
390	func (engine *Engine) Routes() (routes RoutesInfo) {
391		for _, tree := range engine.trees {
392			routes = iterate("", tree.method, routes, tree.root)
393		}
394		return routes
395	}
396	
397	func iterate(path, method string, routes RoutesInfo, root *node) RoutesInfo {
398		path += root.path
399		if len(root.handlers) > 0 {
400			handlerFunc := root.handlers.Last()
401			routes = append(routes, RouteInfo{
402				Method:      method,
403				Path:        path,
404				Handler:     nameOfFunction(handlerFunc),
405				HandlerFunc: handlerFunc,
406			})
407		}
408		for _, child := range root.children {
409			routes = iterate(path, method, routes, child)
410		}
411		return routes
412	}

414	func (engine *Engine) prepareTrustedCIDRs() ([]*net.IPNet, error) {
415		if engine.trustedProxies == nil {
416			return nil, nil
417		}
418	
419		cidr := make([]*net.IPNet, 0, len(engine.trustedProxies))
420		for _, trustedProxy := range engine.trustedProxies {
421			if !strings.Contains(trustedProxy, "/") {
422				ip := parseIP(trustedProxy)
423				if ip == nil {
424					return cidr, &net.ParseError{Type: "IP address", Text: trustedProxy}
425				}
426	
427				switch len(ip) {
428				case net.IPv4len:
429					trustedProxy += "/32"
430				case net.IPv6len:
431					trustedProxy += "/128"
432				}
433			}
434			_, cidrNet, err := net.ParseCIDR(trustedProxy)
435			if err != nil {
436				return cidr, err
437			}
438			cidr = append(cidr, cidrNet)
439		}
440		return cidr, nil
441	}

443	// SetTrustedProxies set a list of network origins (IPv4 addresses,
444	// IPv4 CIDRs, IPv6 addresses or IPv6 CIDRs) from which to trust
445	// request's headers that contain alternative client IP when
446	// `(*gin.Engine).ForwardedByClientIP` is `true`. `TrustedProxies`
447	// feature is enabled by default, and it also trusts all proxies
448	// by default. If you want to disable this feature, use
449	// Engine.SetTrustedProxies(nil), then Context.ClientIP() will
450	// return the remote address directly.
451	func (engine *Engine) SetTrustedProxies(trustedProxies []string) error {
452		engine.trustedProxies = trustedProxies
453		return engine.parseTrustedProxies()
454	}

456	// isUnsafeTrustedProxies checks if Engine.trustedCIDRs contains all IPs, it's not safe if it has (returns true)
457	func (engine *Engine) isUnsafeTrustedProxies() bool {
458		return engine.isTrustedProxy(net.ParseIP("0.0.0.0")) || engine.isTrustedProxy(net.ParseIP("::"))
459	}
460	
461	// parseTrustedProxies parse Engine.trustedProxies to Engine.trustedCIDRs
462	func (engine *Engine) parseTrustedProxies() error {
463		trustedCIDRs, err := engine.prepareTrustedCIDRs()
464		engine.trustedCIDRs = trustedCIDRs
465		return err
466	}
467	
468	// isTrustedProxy will check whether the IP address is included in the trusted list according to Engine.trustedCIDRs
469	func (engine *Engine) isTrustedProxy(ip net.IP) bool {
470		if engine.trustedCIDRs == nil {
471			return false
472		}
473		for _, cidr := range engine.trustedCIDRs {
474			if cidr.Contains(ip) {
475				return true
476			}
477		}
478		return false
479	}
480	
481	// validateHeader will parse X-Forwarded-For header and return the trusted client IP address
482	func (engine *Engine) validateHeader(header string) (clientIP string, valid bool) {
483		if header == "" {
484			return "", false
485		}
486		items := strings.Split(header, ",")
487		for i := len(items) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
488			ipStr := strings.TrimSpace(items[i])
489			ip := net.ParseIP(ipStr)
490			if ip == nil {
491				break
492			}
493	
494			// X-Forwarded-For is appended by proxy
495			// Check IPs in reverse order and stop when find untrusted proxy
496			if (i == 0) || (!engine.isTrustedProxy(ip)) {
497				return ipStr, true
498			}
499		}
500		return "", false
501	}

503	// updateRouteTree do update to the route tree recursively
504	func updateRouteTree(n *node) {
505		n.path = strings.ReplaceAll(n.path, escapedColon, colon)
506		n.fullPath = strings.ReplaceAll(n.fullPath, escapedColon, colon)
507		n.indices = strings.ReplaceAll(n.indices, backslash, colon)
508		if n.children == nil {
509			return
510		}
511		for _, child := range n.children {
512			updateRouteTree(child)
513		}
514	}
515	
516	// updateRouteTrees do update to the route trees
517	func (engine *Engine) updateRouteTrees() {
518		for _, tree := range engine.trees {
519			updateRouteTree(tree.root)
520		}
521	}

523	// parseIP parse a string representation of an IP and returns a net.IP with the
524	// minimum byte representation or nil if input is invalid.
525	func parseIP(ip string) net.IP {
526		parsedIP := net.ParseIP(ip)
527	
528		if ipv4 := parsedIP.To4(); ipv4 != nil {
529			// return ip in a 4-byte representation
530			return ipv4
531		}
532	
533		// return ip in a 16-byte representation or nil
534		return parsedIP
535	}

537	// Run attaches the router to a http.Server and starts listening and serving HTTP requests.
538	// It is a shortcut for http.ListenAndServe(addr, router)
539	// Note: this method will block the calling goroutine indefinitely unless an error happens.
540	func (engine *Engine) Run(addr ...string) (err error) {
541		defer func() { debugPrintError(err) }()
542	
543		if engine.isUnsafeTrustedProxies() {
544			debugPrint("[WARNING] You trusted all proxies, this is NOT safe. We recommend you to set a value.\n" +
545				"Please check https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin/blob/master/docs/doc.md#dont-trust-all-proxies for details.")
546		}
547		engine.updateRouteTrees()
548		address := resolveAddress(addr)
549		debugPrint("Listening and serving HTTP on %s\n", address)
550		server := &http.Server{ // #nosec G112
551			Addr:    address,
552			Handler: engine.Handler(),
553		}
554		err = server.ListenAndServe()
555		return
556	}

578	// RunUnix attaches the router to a http.Server and starts listening and serving HTTP requests
579	// through the specified unix socket (i.e. a file).
580	// Note: this method will block the calling goroutine indefinitely unless an error happens.
581	func (engine *Engine) RunUnix(file string) (err error) {
582		debugPrint("Listening and serving HTTP on unix:/%s", file)
583		defer func() { debugPrintError(err) }()
584	
585		if engine.isUnsafeTrustedProxies() {
586			debugPrint("[WARNING] You trusted all proxies, this is NOT safe. We recommend you to set a value.\n" +
587				"Please check https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin/blob/master/docs/doc.md#dont-trust-all-proxies for details.")
588		}
589	
590		listener, err := net.Listen("unix", file)
591		if err != nil {
592			return
593		}
594		defer listener.Close()
595		defer os.Remove(file)
596	
597		server := &http.Server{ // #nosec G112
598			Handler: engine.Handler(),
599		}
600		err = server.Serve(listener)
601		return
602	}

604	// RunFd attaches the router to a http.Server and starts listening and serving HTTP requests
605	// through the specified file descriptor.
606	// Note: this method will block the calling goroutine indefinitely unless an error happens.
607	func (engine *Engine) RunFd(fd int) (err error) {
608		debugPrint("Listening and serving HTTP on fd@%d", fd)
609		defer func() { debugPrintError(err) }()
610	
611		if engine.isUnsafeTrustedProxies() {
612			debugPrint("[WARNING] You trusted all proxies, this is NOT safe. We recommend you to set a value.\n" +
613				"Please check https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin/blob/master/docs/doc.md#dont-trust-all-proxies for details.")
614		}
615	
616		f := os.NewFile(uintptr(fd), fmt.Sprintf("fd@%d", fd))
617		defer f.Close()
618		listener, err := net.FileListener(f)
619		if err != nil {
620			return
621		}
622		defer listener.Close()
623		err = engine.RunListener(listener)
624		return
625	}
626	
627	// RunQUIC attaches the router to a http.Server and starts listening and serving QUIC requests.
628	// It is a shortcut for http3.ListenAndServeQUIC(addr, certFile, keyFile, router)
629	// Note: this method will block the calling goroutine indefinitely unless an error happens.
630	func (engine *Engine) RunQUIC(addr, certFile, keyFile string) (err error) {
631		debugPrint("Listening and serving QUIC on %s\n", addr)
632		defer func() { debugPrintError(err) }()
633	
634		if engine.isUnsafeTrustedProxies() {
635			debugPrint("[WARNING] You trusted all proxies, this is NOT safe. We recommend you to set a value.\n" +
636				"Please check https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin/blob/master/docs/doc.md#dont-trust-all-proxies for details.")
637		}
638	
639		err = http3.ListenAndServeQUIC(addr, certFile, keyFile, engine.Handler())
640		return
641	}
642	
643	// RunListener attaches the router to a http.Server and starts listening and serving HTTP requests
644	// through the specified net.Listener
645	func (engine *Engine) RunListener(listener net.Listener) (err error) {
646		debugPrint("Listening and serving HTTP on listener what's bind with address@%s", listener.Addr())
647		defer func() { debugPrintError(err) }()
648	
649		if engine.isUnsafeTrustedProxies() {
650			debugPrint("[WARNING] You trusted all proxies, this is NOT safe. We recommend you to set a value.\n" +
651				"Please check https://github.com/gin-gonic/gin/blob/master/docs/doc.md#dont-trust-all-proxies for details.")
652		}
653	
654		server := &http.Server{ // #nosec G112
655			Handler: engine.Handler(),
656		}
657		err = server.Serve(listener)
658		return
659	}
660	
661	// ServeHTTP conforms to the http.Handler interface.
662	func (engine *Engine) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
663		engine.routeTreesUpdated.Do(func() {
664			engine.updateRouteTrees()
665		})
666	
667		c := engine.pool.Get().(*Context)
668		c.writermem.reset(w)
669		c.Request = req
670		c.reset()
671	
672		engine.handleHTTPRequest(c)
673	
674		engine.pool.Put(c)
675	}
676	
677	// HandleContext re-enters a context that has been rewritten.
678	// This can be done by setting c.Request.URL.Path to your new target.
679	// Disclaimer: You can loop yourself to deal with this, use wisely.
680	func (engine *Engine) HandleContext(c *Context) {
681		oldIndexValue := c.index
682		oldHandlers := c.handlers
683		c.reset()
684		engine.handleHTTPRequest(c)
685	
686		c.index = oldIndexValue
687		c.handlers = oldHandlers
688	}
689	
690	func (engine *Engine) handleHTTPRequest(c *Context) {
691		httpMethod := c.Request.Method
692		rPath := c.Request.URL.Path
693		unescape := false
694	
695		if engine.UseEscapedPath {
696			rPath = c.Request.URL.EscapedPath()
697			unescape = engine.UnescapePathValues
698		} else if engine.UseRawPath && len(c.Request.URL.RawPath) > 0 {
699			rPath = c.Request.URL.RawPath
700			unescape = engine.UnescapePathValues
701		}
702	
703		if engine.RemoveExtraSlash {
704			rPath = cleanPath(rPath)
705		}
706	
707		// Find root of the tree for the given HTTP method
708		t := engine.trees
709		for i, tl := 0, len(t); i < tl; i++ {
710			if t[i].method != httpMethod {
711				continue
712			}
713			root := t[i].root
714			// Find route in tree
715			value := root.getValue(rPath, c.params, c.skippedNodes, unescape)
716			if value.params != nil {
717				c.Params = *value.params
718			}
719			if value.handlers != nil {
720				c.handlers = value.handlers
721				c.fullPath = value.fullPath
722				c.Next()
723				c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
724				return
725			}
726			if httpMethod != http.MethodConnect && rPath != "/" {
727				if value.tsr && engine.RedirectTrailingSlash {
728					redirectTrailingSlash(c)
729					return
730				}
731				if engine.RedirectFixedPath && redirectFixedPath(c, root, engine.RedirectFixedPath) {
732					return
733				}
734			}
735			break
736		}
737	
738		if engine.HandleMethodNotAllowed && len(t) > 0 {
739			// According to RFC 7231 section 6.5.5, MUST generate an Allow header field in response
740			// containing a list of the target resource's currently supported methods.
741			allowed := make([]string, 0, len(t)-1)
742			for _, tree := range engine.trees {
743				if tree.method == httpMethod {
744					continue
745				}
746				if value := tree.root.getValue(rPath, nil, c.skippedNodes, unescape); value.handlers != nil {
747					allowed = append(allowed, tree.method)
748				}
749			}
750			if len(allowed) > 0 {
751				c.handlers = engine.allNoMethod
752				c.writermem.Header().Set("Allow", strings.Join(allowed, ", "))
753				serveError(c, http.StatusMethodNotAllowed, default405Body)
754				return
755			}
756		}
757	
758		c.handlers = engine.allNoRoute
759		serveError(c, http.StatusNotFound, default404Body)
760	}
761	
762	var mimePlain = []string{MIMEPlain}
763	
764	func serveError(c *Context, code int, defaultMessage []byte) {
765		c.writermem.status = code
766		c.Next()
767		if c.writermem.Written() {
768			return
769		}
770		if c.writermem.Status() == code {
771			c.writermem.Header()["Content-Type"] = mimePlain
772			_, err := c.Writer.Write(defaultMessage)
773			if err != nil {
774				debugPrint("cannot write message to writer during serve error: %v", err)
775			}
776			return
777		}
778		c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
779	}
780	
781	func redirectTrailingSlash(c *Context) {
782		req := c.Request
783		p := req.URL.Path
784		if prefix := path.Clean(c.Request.Header.Get("X-Forwarded-Prefix")); prefix != "." {
785			prefix = sanitizePathChars(prefix)
786			prefix = removeRepeatedChar(prefix, '/')
787	
788			p = prefix + "/" + req.URL.Path
789		}
790		req.URL.Path = p + "/"
791		if length := len(p); length > 1 && p[length-1] == '/' {
792			req.URL.Path = p[:length-1]
793		}
794		redirectRequest(c)
795	}
796	
797	// sanitizePathChars removes unsafe characters from path strings,
798	// keeping only ASCII letters, ASCII numbers, forward slashes, and hyphens.
799	func sanitizePathChars(s string) string {
800		return strings.Map(func(r rune) rune {
801			if (r >= 'a' && r <= 'z') || (r >= 'A' && r <= 'Z') || (r >= '0' && r <= '9') || r == '/' || r == '-' {
802				return r
803			}
804			return -1
805		}, s)
806	}
807	
808	func redirectFixedPath(c *Context, root *node, trailingSlash bool) bool {
809		req := c.Request
810		rPath := req.URL.Path
811	
812		if fixedPath, ok := root.findCaseInsensitivePath(cleanPath(rPath), trailingSlash); ok {
813			req.URL.Path = bytesconv.BytesToString(fixedPath)
814			redirectRequest(c)
815			return true
816		}
817		return false
818	}
819	
820	func redirectRequest(c *Context) {
821		req := c.Request
822		rPath := req.URL.Path
823		rURL := req.URL.String()
824	
825		code := http.StatusMovedPermanently // Permanent redirect, request with GET method
826		if req.Method != http.MethodGet {
827			code = http.StatusTemporaryRedirect
828		}
829		debugPrint("redirecting request %d: %s --> %s", code, rPath, rURL)
830		http.Redirect(c.Writer, req, rURL, code)
831		c.writermem.WriteHeaderNow()
832	}

103	func (c *Context) reset() {
104		c.Writer = &c.writermem
105		c.Params = c.Params[:0]
106		c.handlers = nil
107		c.index = -1

120	// Copy returns a copy of the current context that can be safely used outside the request's scope.

5	package gin
6	
7	import (
8		"net/http"
9		"path"
10		"regexp"
11		"strings"
12	)
13	
14	var (
15		// regEnLetter matches english letters for http method name
16		regEnLetter = regexp.MustCompile("^[A-Z]+$")
17	
18		// anyMethods for RouterGroup Any method
19		anyMethods = []string{
20			http.MethodGet, http.MethodPost, http.MethodPut, http.MethodPatch,
21			http.MethodHead, http.MethodOptions, http.MethodDelete, http.MethodConnect,
22			http.MethodTrace,
23		}
24	)
25	
26	// IRouter defines all router handle interface includes single and group router.
27	type IRouter interface {
28		IRoutes
29		Group(string, ...HandlerFunc) *RouterGroup
30	}
31	
32	// IRoutes defines all router handle interface.
33	type IRoutes interface {
34		Use(...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
35	
36		Handle(string, string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
37		Any(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
38		GET(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
39		POST(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
40		DELETE(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
41		PATCH(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
42		PUT(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
43		OPTIONS(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
44		HEAD(string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
45		Match([]string, string, ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes
46	
47		StaticFile(string, string) IRoutes
48		StaticFileFS(string, string, http.FileSystem) IRoutes
49		Static(string, string) IRoutes
50		StaticFS(string, http.FileSystem) IRoutes
51	}
52	
53	// RouterGroup is used internally to configure router, a RouterGroup is associated with
54	// a prefix and an array of handlers (middleware).
55	type RouterGroup struct {
56		Handlers HandlersChain
57		basePath string
58		engine   *Engine
59		root     bool
60	}
61	
62	var _ IRouter = (*RouterGroup)(nil)
63	
64	// Use adds middleware to the group, see example code in GitHub.
65	func (group *RouterGroup) Use(middleware ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
66		group.Handlers = append(group.Handlers, middleware...)
67		return group.returnObj()
68	}
69	
70	// Group creates a new router group. You should add all the routes that have common middlewares or the same path prefix.
71	// For example, all the routes that use a common middleware for authorization could be grouped.
72	func (group *RouterGroup) Group(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) *RouterGroup {
73		return &RouterGroup{
74			Handlers: group.combineHandlers(handlers),
75			basePath: group.calculateAbsolutePath(relativePath),
76			engine:   group.engine,
77		}
78	}
79	
80	// BasePath returns the base path of router group.
81	// For example, if v := router.Group("/rest/n/v1/api"), v.BasePath() is "/rest/n/v1/api".
82	func (group *RouterGroup) BasePath() string {
83		return group.basePath
84	}
85	
86	func (group *RouterGroup) handle(httpMethod, relativePath string, handlers HandlersChain) IRoutes {
87		absolutePath := group.calculateAbsolutePath(relativePath)
88		handlers = group.combineHandlers(handlers)
89		group.engine.addRoute(httpMethod, absolutePath, handlers)
90		return group.returnObj()
91	}
92	
93	// Handle registers a new request handle and middleware with the given path and method.
94	// The last handler should be the real handler, the other ones should be middleware that can and should be shared among different routes.
95	// See the example code in GitHub.
96	//
97	// For GET, POST, PUT, PATCH and DELETE requests the respective shortcut
98	// functions can be used.
99	//
100	// This function is intended for bulk loading and to allow the usage of less
101	// frequently used, non-standardized or custom methods (e.g. for internal
102	// communication with a proxy).
103	func (group *RouterGroup) Handle(httpMethod, relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
104		if matched := regEnLetter.MatchString(httpMethod); !matched {
105			panic("http method " + httpMethod + " is not valid")
106		}
107		return group.handle(httpMethod, relativePath, handlers)
108	}
109	
110	// POST is a shortcut for router.Handle("POST", path, handlers).
111	func (group *RouterGroup) POST(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
112		return group.handle(http.MethodPost, relativePath, handlers)
113	}
114	
115	// GET is a shortcut for router.Handle("GET", path, handlers).
116	func (group *RouterGroup) GET(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
117		return group.handle(http.MethodGet, relativePath, handlers)
118	}
119	
120	// DELETE is a shortcut for router.Handle("DELETE", path, handlers).
121	func (group *RouterGroup) DELETE(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
122		return group.handle(http.MethodDelete, relativePath, handlers)
123	}
124	
125	// PATCH is a shortcut for router.Handle("PATCH", path, handlers).
126	func (group *RouterGroup) PATCH(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
127		return group.handle(http.MethodPatch, relativePath, handlers)
128	}
129	
130	// PUT is a shortcut for router.Handle("PUT", path, handlers).
131	func (group *RouterGroup) PUT(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
132		return group.handle(http.MethodPut, relativePath, handlers)
133	}
134	
135	// OPTIONS is a shortcut for router.Handle("OPTIONS", path, handlers).
136	func (group *RouterGroup) OPTIONS(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
137		return group.handle(http.MethodOptions, relativePath, handlers)
138	}
139	
140	// HEAD is a shortcut for router.Handle("HEAD", path, handlers).
141	func (group *RouterGroup) HEAD(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
142		return group.handle(http.MethodHead, relativePath, handlers)
143	}
144	
145	// Any registers a route that matches all the HTTP methods.
146	// GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS, DELETE, CONNECT, TRACE.
147	func (group *RouterGroup) Any(relativePath string, handlers ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
148		for _, method := range anyMethods {
149			group.handle(method, relativePath, handlers)
150		}

31	// RecoveryFunc defines the function passable to CustomRecovery.
32	type RecoveryFunc func(c *Context, err any)
33	
34	// Recovery returns a middleware that recovers from any panics and writes a 500 if there was one.
35	func Recovery() HandlerFunc {
36		return RecoveryWithWriter(DefaultErrorWriter)
37	}
38	
39	// CustomRecovery returns a middleware that recovers from any panics and calls the provided handle func to handle it.
40	func CustomRecovery(handle RecoveryFunc) HandlerFunc {
41		return RecoveryWithWriter(DefaultErrorWriter, handle)
42	}
43	
44	// RecoveryWithWriter returns a middleware for a given writer that recovers from any panics and writes a 500 if there was one.
45	func RecoveryWithWriter(out io.Writer, recovery ...RecoveryFunc) HandlerFunc {
46		if len(recovery) > 0 {
47			return CustomRecoveryWithWriter(out, recovery[0])
48		}
49		return CustomRecoveryWithWriter(out, defaultHandleRecovery)
50	}
51	
52	// CustomRecoveryWithWriter returns a middleware for a given writer that recovers from any panics and calls the provided handle func to handle it.
53	func CustomRecoveryWithWriter(out io.Writer, handle RecoveryFunc) HandlerFunc {
54		var logger *log.Logger
55		if out != nil {
56			logger = log.New(out, "\n\n\x1b[31m", log.LstdFlags)
57		}
58		return func(c *Context) {
59			defer func() {
60				if rec := recover(); rec != nil {
61					// Check for a broken connection, as it is not really a
62					// condition that warrants a panic stack trace.
63					var isBrokenPipe bool
64					err, ok := rec.(error)
65					if ok {
66						isBrokenPipe = errors.Is(err, syscall.EPIPE) ||
67							errors.Is(err, syscall.ECONNRESET) ||
68							errors.Is(err, http.ErrAbortHandler)
69					}
70					if logger != nil {
71						if isBrokenPipe {
72							logger.Printf("%s\n%s%s", rec, secureRequestDump(c.Request), reset)
73						} else if IsDebugging() {
74							logger.Printf("[Recovery] %s panic recovered:\n%s\n%s\n%s%s",
75								timeFormat(time.Now()), secureRequestDump(c.Request), rec, stack(stackSkip), reset)
76						} else {
77							logger.Printf("[Recovery] %s panic recovered:\n%s\n%s%s",
78								timeFormat(time.Now()), rec, stack(stackSkip), reset)
79						}
80					}
81					if isBrokenPipe {
82						// If the connection is dead, we can't write a status to it.
83						c.Error(err) //nolint: errcheck
84						c.Abort()
85					} else {
86						handle(c, rec)
87					}
88				}
89			}()
90			c.Next()
91		}
92	}
93

95	// if present, is replaced with a masked value ("Authorization: *") to avoid leaking sensitive credentials.
96	//
97	// Currently, only the Authorization header is sanitized. All other headers and request data remain unchanged.
98	func secureRequestDump(r *http.Request) string {
99		httpRequest, _ := httputil.DumpRequest(r, false)
100		lines := strings.Split(bytesconv.BytesToString(httpRequest), "\r\n")

5	package gin

12

14	)
15	
16	// EnvGinMode indicates environment name for gin mode.
17	const EnvGinMode = "GIN_MODE"
18	
19	const (
20		// DebugMode indicates gin mode is debug.
21		DebugMode = "debug"
22		// ReleaseMode indicates gin mode is release.
23		ReleaseMode = "release"
24		// TestMode indicates gin mode is test.
25		TestMode = "test"
26	)
27	
28	const (
29		debugCode = iota
30		releaseCode
31		testCode
32	)
33	
34	// DefaultWriter is the default io.Writer used by Gin for debug output and
35	// middleware output like Logger() or Recovery().
36	// Note that both Logger and Recovery provides custom ways to configure their
37	// output io.Writer.
38	// To support coloring in Windows use:
39	//
40	//	import "github.com/mattn/go-colorable"
41	//	gin.DefaultWriter = colorable.NewColorableStdout()
42	var DefaultWriter io.Writer = os.Stdout
43	
44	// DefaultErrorWriter is the default io.Writer used by Gin to debug errors
45	var DefaultErrorWriter io.Writer = os.Stderr
46	
47	var (
48		ginMode  int32 = debugCode
49		modeName atomic.Value
50	)
51	
52	func init() {
53		mode := os.Getenv(EnvGinMode)
54		SetMode(mode)
55	}
56	
57	// SetMode sets gin mode according to input string.
58	func SetMode(value string) {
59		if value == "" {
60			if flag.Lookup("test.v") != nil {
61				value = TestMode
62			} else {
63				value = DebugMode
64			}
65		}
66	
67		switch value {
68		case DebugMode:
69			atomic.StoreInt32(&ginMode, debugCode)
70		case ReleaseMode:
71			atomic.StoreInt32(&ginMode, releaseCode)
72		case TestMode:
73			atomic.StoreInt32(&ginMode, testCode)
74		default:
75			panic("gin mode unknown: " + value + " (available mode: debug release test)")
76		}
77		modeName.Store(value)
78	}
79	
80	// DisableBindValidation closes the default validator.
81	func DisableBindValidation() {
82		binding.Validator = nil
83	}
84	
85	// EnableJsonDecoderUseNumber sets true for binding.EnableDecoderUseNumber to
86	// call the UseNumber method on the JSON Decoder instance.
87	func EnableJsonDecoderUseNumber() {
88		binding.EnableDecoderUseNumber = true
89	}
90	
91	// EnableJsonDecoderDisallowUnknownFields sets true for binding.EnableDecoderDisallowUnknownFields to
92	// call the DisallowUnknownFields method on the JSON Decoder instance.
93	func EnableJsonDecoderDisallowUnknownFields() {
94		binding.EnableDecoderDisallowUnknownFields = true
95	}
96	
97	// Mode returns current gin mode.
98	func Mode() string {
99		return modeName.Load().(string)
100	}

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile a detailed structured report with all the key information:

## Structured Report: Gin HTTP Framework - Logging, Middleware, and Request Lifecycle

### 1. EXISTING LOGGING INFRASTRUCTURE

#### A. Global Writer Variables (mode.go: 34-45)
- **`DefaultWriter`** (line 42): `io.Writer = os.Stdout` - default output for debug/middleware logs
- **`DefaultErrorWriter`** (line 45): `io.Writer = os.Stderr` - default output for error logs

#### B. Logger Middleware System (logger.go: 38-314)

**Core Types:**
- **`LoggerConfig`** (lines 39-59): Configuration struct with:
  - `Formatter` - custom log formatter function
  - `Output` - custom io.Writer
  - `SkipPaths` - paths to skip logging
  - `SkipQueryString` - whether to skip query strings
  - `Skip` - custom Skipper function

- **`LogFormatter`** (line 65): Function signature for custom formatting
- **`LogFormatterParams`** (lines 68-91): Data passed to formatter containing:
  - `Request` (*http.Request)
  - `TimeStamp` (time.Time)
  - `StatusCode` (int)
  - `Latency` (time.Duration)
  - `ClientIP` (string)
  - `Method` (string)
  - `Path` (string)
  - `ErrorMessage` (string)
  - `BodySize` (int)
  - `Keys` (map[any]any)
  - `isTerm` (bool) - terminal detection

**Logger Functions:**
- **`Logger()`** (line 224): Returns default logger middleware
- **`LoggerWithFormatter(f LogFormatter)`** (line 229): Custom formatter
- **`LoggerWithWriter(out io.Writer, notlogged ...string)`** (line 237): Custom writer and skip paths
- **`LoggerWithConfig(conf LoggerConfig)`** (line 245): Full configuration

**Default Formatter:** (lines 167-194)
- Color-coded output with ANSI codes
- Status code colors (green=2xx, yellow=4xx, red=5xx)
- Method colors (blue=GET, cyan=POST, yellow=PUT, red=DELETE, green=PATCH)
- Latency colors based on millisecond thresholds

**Error Logger:**
- **`ErrorLogger()`** (line 207): Logs errors of any type
- **`ErrorLoggerT(typ ErrorType)`** (line 212): Type-specific error logging

#### C. Debug Infrastructure (debug.go: 20-114)

**Debug Variables:**
- **`IsDebugging()`** (line 22): Returns true if framework in debug mode
- **`DebugPrintRouteFunc`** (line 27): Custom route debug output
- **`DebugPrintFunc`** (line 30): Custom debug output

**Debug Functions:**
- **`debugPrint(format string, values ...any)`** (line 56): Core debug output, checks `IsDebugging()` and uses `DebugPrintFunc` if set, otherwise writes to `DefaultWriter` with `[GIN-debug]` prefix
- **`debugPrintRoute()`** (line 32): Prints registered routes
- **`debugPrintLoadTemplate()`** (line 44): Prints loaded HTML templates
- **`debugPrintError(err error)`** (line 110): Prints errors to `DefaultErrorWriter`

#### D. Recovery Middleware (recovery.go: 25-92)

**Functions:**
- **`Recovery()`** (line 35): Default recovery middleware
- **`CustomRecovery(handle RecoveryFunc)`** (line 40): Custom recovery handler
- **`RecoveryWithWriter(out io.Writer, recovery ...RecoveryFunc)`** (line 45): Custom writer
- **`CustomRecoveryWithWriter(out io.Writer, handle RecoveryFunc)`** (line 53): Full configuration

**Features:**
- Uses `log.New()` with custom writer and `[GIN]` prefix (line 56)
- Detects broken connections (line 66-68)
- Conditional stack trace output based on `IsDebugging()` (line 73-78)
- Calls `c.Error()` to attach error to context (line 83)
- Calls `c.Abort()` to stop handler chain (line 84)

---

### 2. MIDDLEWARE SYSTEM

#### A. Middleware Registration (routergroup.go: 64-68, gin.go: 340-345)

**Engine.Use()** (gin.go: 340-345):
```
func (engine *Engine) Use(middleware ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
    engine.RouterGroup.Use(middleware...)
    engine.rebuild404Handlers()
    engine.rebuild405Handlers()
    return engine
}
```

**RouterGroup.Use()** (routergroup.go: 65-68):
```
func (group *RouterGroup) Use(middleware ...HandlerFunc) IRoutes {
    group.Handlers = append(group.Handlers, middleware...)
    return group.returnObj()
}
```

#### B. Handler Chain Combination (routergroup.go: 241-248)

**combineHandlers()** (lines 241-248):
- Combines group middleware + route-specific handlers
- Validates total handlers < `abortIndex` (127, see context.go: 57)
- Order: [Group Middleware...] + [Route Handlers...]

#### C. Middleware Execution Flow (context.go: 188-196)

**Context.Next()** (lines 188-196):
- Increments `c.index++` at each step
- Loops while `index < len(handlers)`
- Calls each handler with the context
- Handlers can call `c.Next()` to continue chain

**Handler Chain Structure:**
- Each request has a `handlers` slice
- `index` tracks current position (-1 initially, incremented by `Next()`)
- `abortIndex` (127) stops further execution

---

### 3. REQUEST LIFECYCLE

#### A. ServeHTTP Entry Point (gin.go: 661-675)

```
func (engine *Engine) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    // 1. Ensure route trees updated (once)
    engine.routeTreesUpdated.Do(func() {
        engine.updateRouteTrees()
    })
    
    // 2. Get Context from pool
    c := engine.pool.Get().(*Context)
    
    // 3. Initialize context with response writer and request
    c.writermem.reset(w)
    c.Request = req
    c.reset()
    
    // 4. Handle HTTP request
    engine.handleHTTPRequest(c)
    
    // 5. Return context to pool
    engine.pool.Put(c)
}
```

**Key Line References:**
- ServeHTTP entry: gin.go: 662
- Context pool Get: gin.go: 667
- WriterMem reset: gin.go: 668
- Request assignment: gin.go: 669
- Context reset: gin.go: 670
- Handler invocation: gin.go: 672
- Context pool Put: gin.go: 674

#### B. HTTP Request Handling (gin.go: 690-760)

**handleHTTPRequest()** (lines 690-760):

1. **Extract request path** (lines 691-705):
   - Get HTTP method: `c.Request.Method` (line 691)
   - Get URL path from `c.Request.URL.Path` (line 692)
   - Handle escaped/raw paths (lines 695-701)
   - Clean extra slashes if enabled (line 704)

2. **Route matching** (lines 707-735):
   - Iterate through method trees (line 709)
   - Call `root.getValue()` to find matching route (line 715)
   - Set `c.Params` from matched parameters (lines 716-718)
   - Set `c.handlers` and `c.fullPath` (lines 720-721)
   - **Call c.Next()** to execute middleware chain (line 722)
   - Write headers (line 723)

3. **Handle 404/405** (lines 738-759):
   - Check method not allowed if enabled (line 738)
   - Redirect trailing slash if enabled (lines 727-730)
   - Redirect fixed path if enabled (line 731)
   - Set `c.handlers` to `allNoMethod` or `allNoRoute` (lines 751, 758)
   - Call `serveError()` (lines 753, 759)

#### C. Middleware Chain Execution (context.go: 188-196)

When `c.Next()` is called:
- Loop increments index through handlers
- Each handler called sequentially: `c.handlers[c.index](c)`
- Handler can call `c.Next()` to continue, or `c.Abort()` to stop

**Abort Control** (context.go: 56-57, 207-209):
```
const abortIndex int8 = math.MaxInt8 >> 1  // 127
func (c *Context) Abort() {
    c.index = abortIndex  // Stops further handlers
}
```

#### D. Default Engine Initialization (gin.go: 235-241)

```
func Default(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
    debugPrintWARNINGDefault()
    engine := New()
    engine.Use(Logger(), Recovery())  // Attached by default
    return engine.With(opts...)
}
```

**Default middleware:** Logger() + Recovery() (line 239)

---

### 4. CONTEXT TYPE & REQUEST DATA

#### A. Context Structure (context.go: 61-97)

```go
type Context struct {
    writermem responseWriter       // Response writer (line 62)
    Request   *http.Request       // HTTP request object (line 63)
    Writer    ResponseWriter      // Public response writer interface (line 64)
    
    Params   Params               // URL parameters (line 66)
    handlers HandlersChain        // Current middleware chain (line 67)
    index    int8                 // Current handler index (line 68)
    fullPath string               // Matched route pattern (line 69)
    
    engine       *Engine          // Reference to engine (line 71)
    params       *Params          // Pointer to params (line 72)
    skippedNodes *[]skippedNode   // Routing tree skipped nodes (line 73)
    
    mu sync.RWMutex              // Protects Keys map (line 76)
    Keys map[any]any             // Request-scoped key/value store (line 79)
    Errors errorMsgs             // Attached errors (line 82)
    Accepted []string            // Content negotiation (line 85)
    
    queryCache url.Values         // Cached query params (line 88)
    formCache url.Values          // Cached form data (line 92)
    sameSite http.SameSite        // Cookie SameSite attribute (line 96)
}
```

#### B. Request Data Access Methods

**URL Parameters:**
- **`Param(key string)`** (context.go: 503): Get URL parameter by name
- **`Params`** slice (tree.go: 17-25): Slice of `Param` structs with `Key` and `Value`

**Query Parameters:**
- **`Query(key string)`** (context.go: 525): Get first query value
- **`GetQuery(key string)`** (context.go: 554): Get with existence check
- **`QueryArray(key string)`** (context.go: 563): Get all values for key
- **`QueryMap(key string)`** (context.go: 587): Get query params as map
- **`initQueryCache()`** (context.go: 568): Lazy initialization from `c.Request.URL.Query()`

**Form/POST Data:**
- **`PostForm(key string)`** (context.go: 601): Get form value
- **`GetPostForm(key string)`** (context.go: 624): Get with existence check
- **`PostFormArray(key string)`** (context.go: 633): Get all form values
- **`PostFormMap(key string)`** (context.go: 660): Get form as map
- **`initFormCache()`** (context.go: 638): Parse multipart form data
- **`FormFile(name string)`** (context.go: 698): Get uploaded file

**Request Metadata:**
- **`ClientIP()`** (context.go: 975): Get client IP with proxy support
- **`RemoteIP()`** (context.go: 1027): Get remote address IP
- **`ContentType()`** (context.go: 1036): Get Content-Type header
- **`GetHeader(key string)`** (context.go: 1089): Get header value
- **`IsWebsocket()`** (context.go: 1042): Check for WebSocket upgrade
- **`FullPath()`** (context.go: 177): Get matched route pattern
- **`HandlerName()`** (context.go: 149): Get main handler name
- **`HandlerNames()`** (context.go: 155): Get all handler names in chain

**Context-scoped Data (Keys):**
- **`Set(key, value)`** (context.go: 276): Store arbitrary data
- **`Get(key)`** (context.go: 288): Retrieve data with existence check
- **`MustGet(key)`** (context.go: 296): Retrieve or panic
- Type-specific getters: `GetString()`, `GetInt()`, `GetBool()`, etc. (context.go: 310-478)

**Error Management:**
- **`Error(err error)`** (context.go: 252): Attach error to context
- **`c.Errors`** (line 82): List of attached errors
- **`Errors.ByType(typ ErrorType)`** (errors.go: 98): Filter errors by type

#### C. Params Type (tree.go: 16-43)

```go
type Param struct {
    Key   string  // Parameter name (line 18)
    Value string  // Parameter value (line 19)
}

type Params []Param

func (ps Params) Get(name string) (string, bool)
func (ps Params) ByName(name string) string
```

#### D. Response Writer Wrapper (response_writer.go: 49-142)

**ResponseWriter Interface** (lines 23-47):
- Embeds `http.ResponseWriter`, `http.Hijacker`, `http.Flusher`, `http.CloseNotifier`
- Adds methods:
  - `Status()` - get HTTP status code
  - `Size()` - get response body size in bytes
  - `Written()` - check if headers written
  - `WriteString()` - write string to response
  - `WriteHeaderNow()` - force header write

**Internal responseWriter** (lines 49-53):
- Tracks `size` and `status` for response
- Wraps underlying `http.ResponseWriter`

---

### 5. KEY FILES SUMMARY

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| **gin.go** | Engine, routing, request handling | 90-189 (Engine struct), 202-233 (New), 235-241 (Default), 661-675 (ServeHTTP), 690-760 (handleHTTPRequest) |
| **context.go** | Request context, data access | 61-97 (Context struct), 103-118 (reset), 188-196 (Next), 207-209 (Abort), 252-268 (Error), 975-1024 (ClientIP) |
| **logger.go** | Logging middleware | 38-59 (LoggerConfig), 65-91 (LogFormatterParams), 224-314 (Logger functions) |
| **debug.go** | Debug output | 20-30 (variables), 56-70 (debugPrint), 110-114 (debugPrintError) |
| **recovery.go** | Panic recovery | 35-92 (Recovery middleware functions) |
| **routergroup.go** | Middleware registration | 64-68 (Use), 86-91 (handle), 241-248 (combineHandlers) |
| **mode.go** | Global config | 34-45 (DefaultWriter, DefaultErrorWriter), 52-78 (SetMode) |
| **errors.go** | Error types | 15-36 (Error struct), 98-112 (ByType filtering) |
| **tree.go** | URL params | 16-25 (Param, Params types) |
| **response_writer.go** | Response tracking | 23-47 (ResponseWriter interface), 49-142 (responseWriter impl) |

---

### 6. CRITICAL EXECUTION FLOW SUMMARY

```
Request arrives at ServeHTTP()
    ↓
Get Context from pool (gin.go:667)
    ↓
Reset Context (gin.go:670, context.go:103-118)
    ↓
handleHTTPRequest() (gin.go:690)

    │   └─ Handler can call c.Abort() to stop (c.index = 127)
    └─ Continue until index >= len(handlers) or aborted

Response written via c.Writer
    ↓
Context returned to pool (gin.go:674)
```

---

### 7. LOGGING FLOW WITH LINE REFERENCES

```
Logger middleware (logger.go:245-314):
    1. Record start time (line 277)

Default formatter (logger.go:167-194):

```

---

## Comprehensive Request/Operation Logging in Gin

Gin already has a logger middleware (`logger.go`) that captures post-response data. "Comprehensive" logging means extending it with: request IDs for trace correlation, structured JSON output, richer context (body sizes, headers, keys from context), and early-request capture before the handler chain runs.

---

### Files That Need to Change

#### 1. `logger.go` — Core of all changes

**a) Extend `LogFormatterParams` (line 68)**

The struct currently holds 9 fields. Add:

```go
// logger.go:68
type LogFormatterParams struct {
    Request     *http.Request
    TimeStamp   time.Time
    StatusCode  int
    Latency     time.Duration
    ClientIP    string
    Method      string
    Path        string
    ErrorMessage string
    isTerm      bool
    BodySize    int

    // New fields:
    RequestID       string        // trace/correlation ID from context Keys
    RequestBodySize int64         // c.Request.ContentLength
    RawQuery        string        // c.Request.URL.RawQuery (currently stripped)
    UserAgent       string        // c.Request.UserAgent()
    Keys            map[any]any   // already present — expose UserID, TraceID, etc.
}
```

**b) Modify the middleware closure in `LoggerWithConfig` (lines 269–314)**

The current closure captures path and start time before `c.Next()`, then builds params after. Extend both sides:

```go
// logger.go:269 — inside LoggerWithConfig, the returned HandlerFunc
return func(c *Context) {
    // === BEFORE c.Next() (request arrival) ===
    start := time.Now()
    path := c.Request.URL.Path
    raw := c.Request.URL.RawQuery   // NEW: capture query string
    reqBodySize := c.Request.ContentLength // NEW: capture incoming size

    c.Next()  // line 282 — execute middleware + handler chain

    // === AFTER c.Next() (response sent) ===
    // Skip check (line 285 area)
    if conf.Skip != nil && conf.Skip(c) { return }
    if _, ok := skip[path]; ok { return }

    param := LogFormatterParams{
        Request:      c.Request,
        isTerm:       isTerm,
        Keys:         c.Keys,
        TimeStamp:    time.Now(),
        Latency:      time.Since(start),
        ClientIP:     c.ClientIP(),
        Method:       c.Request.Method,
        StatusCode:   c.Writer.Status(),
        ErrorMessage: c.Errors.ByType(ErrorTypePrivate).String(),
        BodySize:     c.Writer.Size(),
        // NEW:
        RequestID:       c.GetString("X-Request-ID"),
        RequestBodySize: reqBodySize,
        RawQuery:        raw,
        UserAgent:       c.Request.UserAgent(),
    }
    if !conf.SkipQueryString && raw != "" {
        param.Path = path + "?" + raw
    } else {
        param.Path = path
    }
    fmt.Fprint(out, formatter(param))
}
```

**c) Add a JSON structured formatter**

Add alongside `defaultLogFormatter` (line 167):

```go
// logger.go — new exported formatter
var JSONLogFormatter LogFormatter = func(p LogFormatterParams) string {
    if p.Latency > time.Minute {
        p.Latency = p.Latency.Truncate(time.Second)
    }
    entry := map[string]any{
        "time":              p.TimeStamp.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
        "status":            p.StatusCode,
        "latency_ms":        p.Latency.Milliseconds(),
        "client_ip":         p.ClientIP,
        "method":            p.Method,
        "path":              p.Path,
        "body_bytes_sent":   p.BodySize,
        "request_body_size": p.RequestBodySize,
        "user_agent":        p.UserAgent,
        "request_id":        p.RequestID,
    }
    if p.ErrorMessage != "" {
        entry["error"] = p.ErrorMessage
    }
    // surface context keys of interest (e.g. user_id set by auth middleware)
    if uid, ok := p.Keys["user_id"]; ok {
        entry["user_id"] = uid
    }
    b, _ := json.Marshal(entry)
    return string(b) + "\n"
}
```

#### 2. New file: `requestid.go`

Gin has no request ID concept today. Add this as a first-class middleware so Logger can read it from `c.Keys`:

```go
// requestid.go
package gin

import "crypto/rand"
import "encoding/hex"

func RequestID() HandlerFunc {
    return func(c *Context) {
        id := c.GetHeader("X-Request-ID")
        if id == "" {
            b := make([]byte, 16)
            rand.Read(b)
            id = hex.EncodeToString(b)
        }
        c.Set("X-Request-ID", id)
        c.Header("X-Request-ID", id)  // echo back to client
        c.Next()
    }
}
```

## Comprehensive Request/Operation Logging in Gin

Gin already has a logger middleware (`logger.go`) that captures post-response data. "Comprehensive" logging means extending it with: request IDs for trace correlation, structured JSON output, richer context (body sizes, headers, keys from context), and early-request capture before the handler chain runs.

---

### Files That Need to Change

#### 1. `logger.go` — Core of all changes

**a) Extend `LogFormatterParams` (line 68)**

The struct currently holds 9 fields. Add:

```go
// logger.go:68
type LogFormatterParams struct {
    Request     *http.Request
    TimeStamp   time.Time
    StatusCode  int
    Latency     time.Duration
    ClientIP    string
    Method      string
    Path        string
    ErrorMessage string
    isTerm      bool
    BodySize    int

    // New fields:
    RequestID       string        // trace/correlation ID from context Keys
    RequestBodySize int64         // c.Request.ContentLength
    RawQuery        string        // c.Request.URL.RawQuery (currently stripped)
    UserAgent       string        // c.Request.UserAgent()
    Keys            map[any]any   // already present — expose UserID, TraceID, etc.
}
```

**b) Modify the middleware closure in `LoggerWithConfig` (lines 269–314)**

The current closure captures path and start time before `c.Next()`, then builds params after. Extend both sides:

```go
// logger.go:269 — inside LoggerWithConfig, the returned HandlerFunc
return func(c *Context) {
    // === BEFORE c.Next() (request arrival) ===
    start := time.Now()
    path := c.Request.URL.Path
    raw := c.Request.URL.RawQuery   // NEW: capture query string
    reqBodySize := c.Request.ContentLength // NEW: capture incoming size

    c.Next()  // line 282 — execute middleware + handler chain

    // === AFTER c.Next() (response sent) ===
    // Skip check (line 285 area)
    if conf.Skip != nil && conf.Skip(c) { return }
    if _, ok := skip[path]; ok { return }

    param := LogFormatterParams{
        Request:      c.Request,
        isTerm:       isTerm,
        Keys:         c.Keys,
        TimeStamp:    time.Now(),
        Latency:      time.Since(start),
        ClientIP:     c.ClientIP(),
        Method:       c.Request.Method,
        StatusCode:   c.Writer.Status(),
        ErrorMessage: c.Errors.ByType(ErrorTypePrivate).String(),
        BodySize:     c.Writer.Size(),
        // NEW:
        RequestID:       c.GetString("X-Request-ID"),
        RequestBodySize: reqBodySize,
        RawQuery:        raw,
        UserAgent:       c.Request.UserAgent(),
    }
    if !conf.SkipQueryString && raw != "" {
        param.Path = path + "?" + raw
    } else {
        param.Path = path
    }
    fmt.Fprint(out, formatter(param))
}
```

**c) Add a JSON structured formatter**

Add alongside `defaultLogFormatter` (line 167):

```go
// logger.go — new exported formatter
var JSONLogFormatter LogFormatter = func(p LogFormatterParams) string {
    if p.Latency > time.Minute {
        p.Latency = p.Latency.Truncate(time.Second)
    }
    entry := map[string]any{
        "time":              p.TimeStamp.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
        "status":            p.StatusCode,
        "latency_ms":        p.Latency.Milliseconds(),
        "client_ip":         p.ClientIP,
        "method":            p.Method,
        "path":              p.Path,
        "body_bytes_sent":   p.BodySize,
        "request_body_size": p.RequestBodySize,
        "user_agent":        p.UserAgent,
        "request_id":        p.RequestID,
    }
    if p.ErrorMessage != "" {
        entry["error"] = p.ErrorMessage
    }
    // surface context keys of interest (e.g. user_id set by auth middleware)
    if uid, ok := p.Keys["user_id"]; ok {
        entry["user_id"] = uid
    }
    b, _ := json.Marshal(entry)
    return string(b) + "\n"
}
```

#### 2. New file: `requestid.go`

Gin has no request ID concept today. Add this as a first-class middleware so Logger can read it from `c.Keys`:

```go
// requestid.go
package gin

import "crypto/rand"
import "encoding/hex"

func RequestID() HandlerFunc {
    return func(c *Context) {
        id := c.GetHeader("X-Request-ID")
        if id == "" {
            b := make([]byte, 16)
            rand.Read(b)
            id = hex.EncodeToString(b)
        }
        c.Set("X-Request-ID", id)
        c.Header("X-Request-ID", id)  // echo back to client
        c.Next()
    }
}
```

**Callee chain for this file:**
- `c.GetHeader()` → `context.go:1089`
- `c.Set()` → `context.go:276` (writes to `c.Keys` under `c.mu` lock)
- `c.Header()` → `context.go` (calls `c.Writer.Header().Set()`)
- `c.Next()` → `context.go:188`

---

#### 3. `gin.go` — Update `Default()` (line 235)

`Default()` at line 239 currently calls `engine.Use(Logger(), Recovery())`. Add `RequestID()` first so Logger can read it:

```go
// gin.go:239
func Default(opts ...OptionFunc) *Engine {
    debugPrintWARNINGDefault()
    engine := New()
    engine.Use(RequestID(), Logger(), Recovery())  // RequestID must come first
    return engine.With(opts...)
}
```

This is a **caller** of both `Logger()` and the new `RequestID()`.

### Request Lifecycle with Logging

```
HTTP request → ServeHTTP() [gin.go:662]
    → pool.Get() Context
    → handleHTTPRequest() [gin.go:690]
        → route match, set c.handlers = [RequestID, Logger, Recovery, YourHandler]
        → c.Next() [gin.go:722]

                captures/generates ID, sets c.Keys["X-Request-ID"], calls c.Next()
            → Logger middleware [logger.go:269]
                records start time, path, raw query, reqBodySize          ← BEFORE
                calls c.Next() [logger.go:282]
                    → Recovery middleware [recovery.go]
                        calls c.Next()
                            → YourHandler runs, writes response
                        catches panics, calls c.Error() [context.go:252]
                reads c.Writer.Status(), c.Writer.Size(), c.Keys           ← AFTER
                calls formatter(param), writes to DefaultWriter
    → pool.Put() Context
```
